sections.category: LLM Workflow > OpenClaw / NemoClaw / Hermes Agents

bullets.body: **OpenClaw 2026.4.14 / 2026.4.15** — Following the 2026.4.14 release on Apr 14, npm picks up **2026.4.15** (about 4 days back). Defaults to Claude Opus 4.7, adds Gemini TTS, model auth status card, cloud-backed LanceDB memory, Copilot embedding, slimmer plugin packaging, and **stronger prompt-injection resistance** (defenses against config-overwrite attacks). Community reviews continue inside the window.

**Hermes Agent v0.10.0 (Apr 16, just outside but with continuing impact)** — 118 bundled skills, 3-tier memory, 6 messaging integrations (Telegram / Discord / Slack / WhatsApp / Signal / email), **closed-loop learning** (autoderives reusable skills from experience). 95.6K → 106.3K stars. Active commits continuing through Apr 21.

citations.label: Hermes Atlas - State of Hermes Agent April 2026

bullets.body: **NemoClaw (NVIDIA)** — The enterprise wrapper around OpenClaw with audit logging / access control / guardrails. No new release inside the window. **NIM-based GPU-exclusive configurations** are the differentiator.

citations.label: Prateek Singh PhD - The Agent Wars: OpenClaw, NemoClaw, Hermes

bullets.body: **v0.19.1 (Apr 18, patch)** — Transformers v5.5.4 upgrade and Gemma4 bug fix on top of v0.19.0. The v0.19.0 capability set carries forward (full Gemma 4 MoE / multimodal / reasoning / tool use, Zero-Bubble Async Scheduling + speculative decoding, mature Model Runner V2, full ViT CUDA-graph support, Transformers v5 compatibility). No new stable inside the window.

**v0.5.10.post1 (Apr 8) is the latest stable** — No new release inside the window. Recent flagship features: piecewise CUDA Graph defaults, Elastic EP (DeepSeek MoE partial-failure resilience), HiSparse sparse attention.

**CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) widens inside the window** — `serving_rerank.py`'s use of `jinja2.Environment()` permits SSTI. A malicious GGUF embeds a Jinja2 payload in `tokenizer.chat_template`, and RCE fires when `/v1/rerank` is called — Qwen3 reranker detection logic is the trigger. The fix is migration to `ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment`.

**Claude Opus 4.7 deployed to 27 AWS regions.** Dynamic capacity allocation in the new inference engine, **Adaptive Thinking** (allocates reasoning-token budget by request complexity), 1M context, zero operator access.

citations.label: AWS Weekly Roundup - Claude Opus 4.7 in Amazon Bedrock (Apr 20)

bullets.body: **Bedrock Cost Attribution** — Auto-attribution of API calls by IAM principal / session tag, linked to CUR 2.0. Visibility by team / project / cost center.

citations.label: AWS - Claude Opus 4.7 now available in Amazon Bedrock

bullets.body: **AWS Interconnect GA** — Multi-cloud private connectivity with "last-mile" options. Backbone for the giant Anthropic-AWS contract.

sections.category: Ecosystems (serving infrastructure) > Alibaba Cloud Model Studio (proprietary API)

bullets.body: **Qwen3.6-Max-Preview (hosted only, released Apr 20)** — Tops **6 major benchmarks**: SWE-bench Pro / SkillsBench / SciCode / Terminal-Bench 2.0 and others. 260K context, preserve_thinking, OpenAI / Anthropic-compatible APIs. Beats Plus by SkillsBench +9.9, SciCode +10.8, Terminal-Bench 2.0 +3.8. **No open weights** — API only, no local execution. Available via Alibaba Cloud Model Studio / Qwen Studio.
**Qwen3.6-Plus (hosted only, proprietary)** — SWE-bench Verified 78.8%, Terminal-Bench 2.0 61.6%, 1M context. BenchLM composite 77/100. Also no local execution; API only.

**Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (open weights, released Apr 16)** — The **only locally runnable variant** in the Qwen3.6 family. 262K context, SWE-bench Verified 73.4%, GPQA 86.0%, AIME 2026 92.7%. Simon Willison's pelican-test win over Claude Opus 4.7 keeps coming back inside the window on r/LocalLLaMA. Day-1 Ollama / llama.cpp support.

citations.label: Hacker News - Qwen3.6-35B-A3B: Agentic coding power, now open to all

bullets.body: **Bonsai-1.7B on CUDA deployment guide** (Dev|Journal, 2026-04-19) — Hands-on coverage of GGUF benchmarks, chat, JSON structured output, and RAG. RTX 4090 hits 674 tok/s (3x FP16). The center of attention inside the window.

citations.label: Dev|Journal - Deploying PrismML Bonsai-1.7B on CUDA

bullets.body: **WebGPU support broadens** — Direct GPU matrix-op access from the browser. More real-device validations of the 290 MB Bonsai 1.7B quantized build.

citations.label: n1n.ai - Local Inference Breakthrough: 1-bit Bonsai WebGPU

bullets.body: **b8838 (Apr 18)** — `llama-cli --endpoint` for remote-server connection, prefix-cache fix for the Anthropic API, request-handling improvements for WebUI thinking mode, verifiable-inference sample (commit-and-open via SHA-256 / Merkle). Apr 20-21 also see continuing server-API cleanup commits.

**v0.21.0 (Apr 17)** — `ollama launch` for **bulk setup of OpenClaw / Hermes / GitHub Copilot CLI**, MLX-based Apple Silicon Gemma 4 perf gains, restored Gemma 4 nothink renderer, ROCm 7.2.1 (Linux), fix to OpenClaw `--yes` non-interactive mode. No new store release inside the window.

sections.category: Ecosystems (local inference runtimes) > Azure Foundry Local

bullets.body: No new announcement inside the window. Comparison articles continue.

citations.label: Knowledge Share - Mastering Azure Foundry Local

bullets.body: **SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8)** — Jinja2 SSTI in `tokenizer.chat_template`, RCE through `/v1/rerank`. Trigger designed around Qwen3 reranker detection logic. Mitigation: migrate to `ImmutableSandboxedEnvironment`.

**Indirect Prompt Injection (IPI) systematized** (Lakera research, Apr 20) — Attack surface includes web pages / PDFs / MCP metadata / RAG documents / email / memory / code — every input the AI ingests. Root cause: trusted and untrusted inputs sharing the same context.
**CIS Report (Apr 1, outside window but referenced through it)** — Lists prompt injection as the top LLM01 risk. Real-world impact: credential theft, unauthorized actions, persistent compromise.

citations.label: CIS - New CIS Report Warns Prompt Injection

bullets.body: **Indirect prompt injection PoC over GitHub Actions (continuing)** — Anthropic Claude Code Security Review, Gemini CLI Action, GitHub Copilot Agent — all three exfiltrate GITHUB_TOKEN. Silently patched without CVE.

citations.label: SecurityWeek - Agents Vulnerable to Prompt Injection via Comments

bullets.body: **CVE-2026-5760** — SGLang, CVSS **9.8**, RCE via Jinja2 SSTI.

**CISA KEV adds (Apr 20, 8 entries)**: - **CVE-2026-20122** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs — vManage privilege capture. [CISA - Adds Eight Known Exploited Vulnerabilities](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2026/04/20/cisa-adds-eight-known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog) - **CVE-2026-20128** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Storing Passwords in a Recoverable Format — DCA user escalation. CVSS 7.5 (reference). [HelpNetSecurity - CISA flags another Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager bug](https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/04/21/cisa-flags-another-cisco-catalyst-sd-wan-manager-bug-as-exploited-cve-2026-20133/) - **CVE-2026-20133** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager Exposure of Sensitive Information. [TheCyberExpress - CISA Adds 8 Flaws](https://thecyberexpress.com/cisa-kev-catalog-vulnerabilities/) - CVE-2023-27351 (PaperCut NG/MF Improper Authentication), CVE-2024-27199 (JetBrains TeamCity Path Traversal), CVE-2025-2749 (Kentico Xperience Path Traversal), CVE-2025-32975 (Quest KACE Improper Authentication), CVE-2025-48700 (Synacor Zimbra XSS) round out the batch. Federal deadline: Apr 23.

citations.label: CISA - Adds Eight Known Exploited Vulnerabilities
HelpNetSecurity - CISA flags another Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager bug

bullets.body: **CVE-2026-20184** Cisco Webex — Authentication bypass enables user impersonation. Emergency advisory in April 2026; no public PoC observed.

**Continuing watch**: CVE-2026-33824 (Windows IKE, 9.8), CVE-2026-33827 (Windows TCP/IP, 8.1-9.8), CVE-2026-33826 (Windows AD, 8.0), CVE-2026-32157 (RDP Client, 8.8), CVE-2026-35616 (Fortinet FortiClient EMS, exploited).

sections.category: Bay Area / Silicon Valley AI Meetups

bullets.body: **AI Tinkerers San Francisco** (monthly, live coding demos, no slides — late-April session announced).
**Bond AI - SF and Bay Area** (Luma, 120K+ members, events inside the window).
**SF AI Meetup** (sfbay-ai) — Recurring talks on GenAI / LLM / Agent practice.
**UNLOCK 2026 (2026-04-22, one-day summit)** — AI × drug discovery / life sciences. Official slots already filled, waitlist running.
**AgentCon San Francisco (2026-05-04 announced)** — A developer conference focused on AI agents.

**Amazon's added $25B investment in Anthropic (Apr 20)** — $5B immediate + $20B milestone-tied. Anthropic commits over $100B to AWS over 10 years. **5 GW** of Trainium2/3, with 1 GW running by year-end. Project Rainier alone is roughly 500K Trainium2 chips.

citations.label: About Amazon - Amazon and Anthropic expand strategic collaboration

bullets.body: **Claude Design (released Apr 17, Anthropic Labs research preview / outside the window but rippling through it)** — Built on Claude Opus 4.7, generates **prototypes / slides / pitch decks / one-pagers** from prompts. Reads team design systems out of codebases or design files, exports to Canva / PPTX / PDF / URL. Positioned against Figma.

**Simon Willison (2026-04-18) "Research: Claude system prompts as a git timeline"** — Converts Anthropic's published system-prompt history to a **git log / diff / blame format** for exploration. Per-model and per-revision files, committed at announcement times.

citations.label: Simon Willison - Research: Claude system prompts as a git timeline

bullets.body: **Simon Willison (2026-04-18) "Changes in the system prompt between Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7"** — Detailed Opus 4.6→4.7 diff: removal of the President Trump annotation (Knowledge cutoff updated to 2026-01), `developer platform` renamed to `Claude Platform`, etc.

citations.label: Simon Willison - Changes in the system prompt between Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7

bullets.body: **Simon Willison (2026-04-19) "Headless everything for personal AI"** — Personal AI is faster and more reliable hitting headless services directly than driving a GUI. Frames an axis against browser-operating agents.

citations.label: Simon Willison - Headless everything for personal AI

bullets.body: **Amazon Bedrock: Claude Mythos Preview** — A gated research preview, a Claude derivative specialized for cybersecurity. Restricted access.

citations.label: InfoQ - Anthropic Releases Claude Mythos Preview

bullets.body: **MIT Technology Review "10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now"** scheduled for 2026-04-21. Industry roundup at the close of the 3-day window, expected to ripple. ---
